source_url: C:\Users\spars\OneDrive\Desktop\FAA\OneDrive_2026-03-20\TC Aircraft Certification Speciality Course 2011\MAY 2011 Binder 1-General\4-Delegation of Authority\Handouts\HO-DA-01 TP 155878.pdf
page_url: C:\Users\spars\OneDrive\Desktop\FAA\OneDrive_2026-03-20\TC Aircraft Certification Speciality Course 2011\MAY 2011 Binder 1-General\4-Delegation of Authority\Handouts\HO-DA-01 TP 155878.pdf#page=5
title: PDF Page 5
content:
c 
, . 
INTRANETSITE 
PoUC}' .. &.REGULATORY SERVICES 
Ministerial Delegation Of Authority #155878 (May 17, 2005) 
SCHEDULE E-7 
Civil Aviation Safety Inspector, Aircraft Certification 
Pursuant to the indicated sections of the Canadian Aviation Regulations. 
1. 103.02(1) To give reasonable notice to the owner or operator of an aircraft to make the aircraft 
available for inspection in accordance with the notice. 
2. 1 03.02(2) To demand that a person who 
(a) is the holder of a Canadian aviation document, 
(b) is the owner, operator or pilot-in-command of an aircraft in respect of which a Canadian aviation 
document, technical record or other document is kept, or 
(c) has in possession a Canadian aviation document, technical record or other document relating to 
an aircraft or a commercial air service 
produce the Canadian aviation document, technical record or other document for inspection in 
accordance with the terms of a demand. 
3. 103.04(b) To give reasonable notice to obtain a printed on paper copy of the records contained in 
recording systems. 
4. 103.06(2) To include in a notice issued pursuant to subsections 7(1) and (2) of the Act 
(a) a statement of the effective date of the suspension; 
(b) a statement of the conditions under which the suspension is terminated; and 
(c) a statement that a request for review by the Tribunal does not operate as a stay of the suspension. 
5.103.06(3) To include in a notice issued pursuant to subsections 7.1(1) and (2) of the Act 
(a) where the Minister has decided to suspend or cancel a Canadian aviation document. a statement 
of the effective date of the suspension or cancellation; 
(b) where the Minister has decided to suspend the Canadian aviation document, a statement of the 
duration of the suspension or the conditions under which the suspension is terminated; and 
(c) a statement that a request for review by the Tribunal does not operate as a stay of the suspension, 
cancellation or refusal to renew. 
5/5/2008http://tcinfo/civilaviation/RegServ/Affairs/DONE 7.htm 
c

source_url: C:\Users\spars\OneDrive\Desktop\FAA\OneDrive_2026-03-20\TC Aircraft Certification Speciality Course 2011\MAY 2011 Binder 1-General\4-Delegation of Authority\Handouts\HO-DA-01 TP 155878.pdf
page_url: C:\Users\spars\OneDrive\Desktop\FAA\OneDrive_2026-03-20\TC Aircraft Certification Speciality Course 2011\MAY 2011 Binder 1-General\4-Delegation of Authority\Handouts\HO-DA-01 TP 155878.pdf#page=6
title: PDF Page 6
content:
lJeJegatIon or Alltl10nty - ::SCHbUULb t,--/ Page 2 of4 
6. 103.07 In addition to the grounds referred to in sections 6.9 to 7.1 of the Act. to suspend. cancel or 
refuse to renew a Canadian aviation document where 
(a) the Canadian aviation document has been voluntarily surrendered by its holder; 
(b) the Canadian aviation document has been mutilated. altered. or rendered illegible; 
(c) the aircraft in respect of which the Canadian aviation document was issued has been destroyed or 
withdrawn from use; or 
(d) the commercial air service. other service or undertaking in respect of which the Canadian aviation 
document was issued has been discontinued. 
7. 103.09 Where anything has been seized pursuant to paragraph 8.7(1 )(c) of the Act. to 
(a) mark it in a clearly identifiable manner; 
(b) take reasonable care to preserve it until it is required to be produced as evidence; and 
(c) return it to the person from whom it was seized within 90 days after the seizure. where 
(i) there is no dispute as to who is lawfully entitled to possession of the thing seized. 
(ii) the retum is not likely to affect aviation safety. and 
(iii) the continued detention of the thing seized is not required for the purposes of an investigation. 
hearing or other similar proceeding. 
8. 103.10 Where an aircraft has been detained pursuant to paragraph 8.7(1 )(d) of the Act. to 
(a) take reasonable care to preserve it; and 
(b) return it to the person who had custody of it when it was detained or to the person who is lawfully 
entitled to possession of it, where there are reasonable grounds to believe that the aircraft 
(i) will not be operated. or 
(Ii) is airworthy or will be rendered airworthy prior to operation and will not be operated in an unsafe 
manner. 
9.202.46(1) To request that the owner of an aircraft inform. in writing, of the location of the aircraft and 
whether or not the aircraft is serviceable. 
Pursuant to the indicated sections of the Aeronautics Act. 
10. Subsection 7.(1) To suspend a Canadian aviation document, other than an Air Operator Certificate 
or a Private Operator Certificate, on the grounds that an immediate threat to aviation safety exists or is 
likely to occur as a result of an act or thing having been. being or proposed to be done under the 
authority of the document. 
11. Subsections 7.(1) and (2) Where he/she decides to suspend a Canadian aviation document. other 
than an Air Operator Certificate or a Private Operator Certificate, on the grounds that an immediate threat 
5/5/2008http;//tcinfo/civilaviationlRegServ/Affairs/DONE7 .htm

source_url: C:\Users\spars\OneDrive\Desktop\FAA\OneDrive_2026-03-20\TC Aircraft Certification Speciality Course 2011\MAY 2011 Binder 1-General\4-Delegation of Authority\Handouts\HO-DA-01 TP 155878.pdf
page_url: C:\Users\spars\OneDrive\Desktop\FAA\OneDrive_2026-03-20\TC Aircraft Certification Speciality Course 2011\MAY 2011 Binder 1-General\4-Delegation of Authority\Handouts\HO-DA-01 TP 155878.pdf#page=7
title: PDF Page 7
content:
to aviation safety exists or is likely to occur as a result of an act or thing having been, being or proposed 
to be done under the authority of the document. forthwith by personal service or by registered or certified 
mail sent to the holder of the document or to the owner or operator of any aircraft, airport or other facility 
in respect of which the document was issued as the case may be, at that person's latest known address, 
to notify the holder, owner or operator of his/her decision by a notice in such form as the Govemor in 
Council may by regulation prescribe and, in addition to any other information that may be so prescribed, 
(a) indicating the immediate threat to aviation safety that he/she believes exists or is likely to occur as 
a result of an act or thing having been, being or proposed to be done under the authority of the 
Canadian aviation document concerned, and the nature of that act or thing; and 
(b) stating the date, being thirty days after the notice is served or sent, on or before which and the 
address at which a request for a review of his/her decision is to be filed in the event the holder of the 
document or the owner or operator concerned wishes to have the decision reviewed. 
12. Paragraphs 7.1(1)(a) (b) and (c) and Subsection (2) In relation to any airworthiness related 
document, other than an Air Operator Certificate or a Private Operator Certificate, to suspend a 
Canadian aviation document, on the grounds that the holder of the document is incompetent or the 
holder or any aircraft or facility in respect of which the document was issued ceases to meet the 
qualifications necessary for the issuance of the document or to fulfil the conditions subject to which the 
document was issued, or to suspend such a document on the grounds that the public interest and, in 
particular. the record in relation to aviation of the holder of the document or any principal of the holder 
warrant it, and by personal service or by registered mail sent to the holder or to the owner or operator of 
the aircraft, or facility, as the case may be, at his latest known address, to notifythat person of his or her 
decision by a notice, in such form as the Governor in Council may by regulations prescribe and. in 
addition to any other information that may be so prescribed, 
(a) indicating. as the case requires, the nature of the incompetence of the holder of the Canadian 
aviation document that he/she believes exists, the qualifications necessary for the issuance of the 
document that he/she believes the holder of the document or the aircraft or facility in respect of which 
the document was issued ceases to have or the conditions subject to which the document was issued 
that he/she believes are no longer being met or complied with and the elements of the public interest 
on which his/her decision is based; and 
(b) stating the date. being thirty days after the notice is served or sent, on or before which and the 
address at which a request for a review of his/her decision is to be filed in the event the holder of the 
document or the owner or operator concerned wishes to have the decision reviewed. 
13. Subsection 8.7(1). Subject to subsection 8.7(4) of the Aeronautics Act: 
(a) to enter any aircraft, aerodrome, facility relating to aeronautics or any premises used for the 
design, manufacture, distribution, maintenance or installation or aeronautical products for the 
purposes of making inspections or audits relating to the enforcement of Part I of that Act; 
(a.1) remove any document or other thing from the place where the inspection or audit is being carried 
out for examination or, in the case of a document, copying; 
(c) to seize anything found in any place referred to in paragraph (a) that he/she believes on 
reasonable grounds will afford evidence with respect to an offence under Part I; and 
(d) to detain any aircraft that he/she believes on reasonable grounds is unsafe or is likely to be 
operated in an unsafe manner and take reasonable steps to ensure its continued detention. 
14. Subsection 8.7(1.1) In carrying out an inspection or audit in any place referred to in paragraph (1)

(a), he or she may: 
(a) use or cause to be used any computer system or data processing system at the place to examine 
any data contained in, or available to, the system; 
(b) reproduce any record, or cause it to be reproduced from the data, in the form of a printout or other 
intelligible output, and remove the printout or other output for examination or copying; and 
(c) use or cause to be used any copying equipment at the place to make copies of any books, 
records. electronic data or other documents.

Ministerial Delegation OfAuthority #155878 
(May 17, 2005) 
SCHEDULE E-1 . 
Director, Aircraft Certification and the person designated to act for him or 
her in his or her absence. 
1. To exercise all the powers set out in Schedules E-2 to E-10 inclusive. 
Pursuant to the indicated sections of the Canadian Aviation Regulations. 
2.103.01(1) To make standards for incorporation by reference into the 
Canadian Aviation Regulations. 
Pursuant to the indicated sections of the Aeronautics Act. 
3. Subsection 5.9(2) On such terms and conditions as he or she 
considers necessary, to exempt any person, class of persons or class of 
aircraft from the application of any provision that requires compliance 
with standards, made pursuant to the CARs for which he or she is 
responsible. if he or she is of the opinion that the exemption is in the 
public interest and is not likely to adversely affect aviation safety. 
4. Paragraphs 7.1(1)(a)(b) and (c) and subsection (2) In relation to an 
airworthiness related document for which he or she is responsible, to 
suspend or cancel a Canadian aviation document, on the grounds that 
the holder of the document is incompetent or the holder or any aircraft. 
airport or other facility in respect of which the document was issued 
ceases to meet the qualifications necessary for the issuance of the 
document, or to fulfil the conditions subject to which the document was 
issued, or to suspend or cancel such a document on the grounds that the 
public interest and. in particular, the record in relation to aviation of the 
holder of the document or any principal of the holder warrant it, and by 
personal service or by registered or certified mail sent to the holder or to 
the owner or operator of the aircraft. airport or facility, as the case may 
be, at his or her latest known address. to notify that person of his or her 
decision by a notice, in such form as the Governor in Council may by 
regulations prescribe and, in addition to any other information that may 
be so prescribed. 
(a) indicating. as the case requires. the nature of the incompetence of 
the holder of the Canadian aviation document that he or she believes 
exists. the qualifications necessary for the issuance of the document 
that he or she believes the holder of the document or the aircraft, 
airport or facility in respect of which the document was issued ceases 
to have or the conditions subject to which the document was issued 
that he or she believes are no longer being met or complied with or
